_notes.purpose: Iter-8 LIVE-DATA variant of us-services.json. Identical in every respect EXCEPT base_sofr.source, which is bound to FRED SOFR at runtime (source.type='api') instead of the static Iter-6 snapshot. This is the first live FRED bind target. It is a SEPARATE profile (distinct applies_to.industry='services-live') rather than an in-place edit of us-services.json, because the Iter-6/7 regression tests (test_composer_derived_domestic.py) resolve us-services.json through the real profile_resolver and must stay OFFLINE-deterministic per the Iter-8 acceptance ('offline regression tests continue to use snapshots'). Flipping the shipped snapshot profile to hard-live would make CI network-dependent. A treasurer wanting live rates selects industry='services-live'; CI and offline runs keep industry='services'.
_notes.hybrid_transform: HYBRID binding (Iter-8 agreed scope): base_sofr.initial is bound LIVE from FRED SOFR latest observation; peak and final remain profile-DECLARED (the V1 anchor 5.50%/4.75%). A Fed dot-plot source for the forward points (peak/final) is a later iteration. The api_binding layer writes only inputs.initial; peak/final/months_to_peak/total_months_assumption stay exactly as declared below and still pass N3c's inputs_schema cross-file check.
_notes.cache_policy_location: cache_policy lives INSIDE base_sofr.source (source is a free-form object in risk-factor-profile.schema.json — no additionalProperties:false on it), so this needs NO schema change. N3c profile_spec_validator does not inspect source at all; it validates inputs against the component inputs_schema, and the live-bound inputs.initial (a real SOFR fraction ~0.04) sits well inside base_sofr's [-0.05, 0.30] bound.
_notes.cache_policy_choice: mode='cached_within_days', max_age_days=4. SOFR publishes on US business days; a 4-day window tolerates a long weekend / holiday gap while still refusing genuinely stale data. On a FRED outage, api_binding falls back to the most recent cached snapshot IF it is within 4 days and stamps freshness='stale, as of <date>'; beyond 4 days (or no snapshot) it is an honest GIVE-UP. To require always-fresh instead, set mode='refresh_on_run' (then ANY outage is an immediate GIVE-UP).
_notes.offline_warning: Resolving THIS profile triggers a live FRED call inside profile_resolver (the api binding step). It is NOT safe for offline CI. The honest-failure test (test_honest_failure.py) exercises the binding with the HTTP layer monkeypatched, never hitting the network. The live smoke test runs on the user's Windows host (api.stlouisfed.org is not in the sandbox allow-list).
_notes.non_base_sofr_components: demand_volatility, payment_cycle, wc are IDENTICAL to us-services.json (still config_file/snapshot). Iter-8 binds ONLY FRED/SOFR; Census/BLS bindings for those components are Iter-9. Held identical so the only behavioural difference vs us-services.json is the live base_sofr.initial.

source.citation: FRED series SOFR (Federal Reserve Bank of New York, retrieved from FRED), latest observation, bound to base_sofr.initial at runtime. peak/final remain the declared V1 anchor pending a Fed dot-plot source (later iteration). The declared inputs.initial=0.0450 above is the offline DECLARED fallback value and the seed for the inputs_schema check; it is OVERWRITTEN by the live FRED value when binding succeeds.
Identical to us-services.json. Live ISM Services PMI binding is Iter-9. Formula spec per design-v1-risk-factor-catalog.md §b.1.
Identical to us-services.json. Live Atradius/Intuit binding is Iter-9. Formula spec per design-v1-risk-factor-catalog.md §b.5.
Identical to us-services.json. Live FRED ISRATIO binding is Iter-9.
